name: Pola de Allande al Puerto de Palo y La Marta – circular en el Camino Primitivo
region_intro: Mountain bike trails Allande are set within the mountainous relief of Asturias, Spain, offering extensive natural spaces for exploration. The region is characterized by rugged terrain, with its highest point being Pico Panchón at 1,410 meters, and is further divided by the Sierra de Los Lagos mountain range. These features provide challenging and scenic routes for mountain bikers. The area also encompasses stages of the Primitive Way of St. James, offering historic paths through diverse landscapes. Best mountain bike trails around Allande The most popular mountain bike trail is Corias - Santuario del Acebo MTB Trail, a 14.0 miles (22.5 km) trail that takes 2 hours 19 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers a rewarding journey through the region's natural beauty. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Pola de Allande al Puerto de Palo y La Marta – circular en el Camino Primitivo, a difficult 29.8 miles (47.9 km) path. This route traverses sections of the historic Primitive Way of St. James, offering varied terrain and cultural insights. Local mountain bikers also love the Cangas del Narcea al Santuario del Acebo – ruta circular, a 24.2 miles (39.0 km) trail leading through mountainous landscapes, often completed in about 4 hours 9 minutes. Mountain biking around Allande is defined by mountainous relief, the Sierra de Los Lagos, and traditional highland pastures known as "Brañas." The network offers options primarily for moderate to difficult ability levels, with 1 moderate and 9 difficult routes available. The routes in Allande are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from 1 review. More than 40 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Allande's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What is the general difficulty level of mountain bike trails in Allande?
A: Mountain biking in Allande is primarily suited for experienced riders. Out of the 10 available routes, 9 are classified as difficult, with only one moderate option. The region's mountainous relief, including the Sierra de Los Lagos, contributes to the challenging terrain.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in Allande?
A: Yes, Allande offers several circular mountain bike routes. For example, you can explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1027482349/pola-de-allande-al-puerto-de-palo-y-la-marta-circular-en-el-camino-primitivo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pola de Allande al Puerto de Palo y La Marta – circular en el Camino Primitivo&lt;/a&gt;, which is a difficult 47.9 km trail traversing sections of the historic Primitive Way of St. James. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1027481557/cangas-del-narcea-al-santuario-del-acebo-ruta-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cangas del Narcea al Santuario del Acebo – ruta circular&lt;/a&gt;, a difficult 39.0 km route through mountainous landscapes.

Q: What kind of natural features can I expect to see while mountain biking in Allande?
A: Allande is characterized by a rugged, mountainous relief, with its highest point being Pico Panchón at 1,410 meters. You'll encounter the Sierra de Los Lagos mountain range and traditional highland pastures known as "Brañas." The region also boasts a Starlight-certified sky, offering unique night-time experiences. For specific natural highlights, consider visiting the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5593878" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pozo de las Mujeres Muertas Pass&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5593872" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Connio Pass&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any waterfalls accessible from the mountain bike trails in Allande?
A: Yes, the region around Allande features beautiful waterfalls. You can find the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2540246" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Oneta Waterfalls&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4999825" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Méxica Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, which are notable natural monuments in the area. While not directly on all MTB trails, they are nearby attractions worth exploring.

Q: What historical or cultural landmarks can I explore near the mountain bike routes?
A: Allande is rich in history. You can visit the ancient Iron Age town of Castro de San Chuis or explore Pola de Allande, recognized as one of Spain's "Magic Towns" with its historic monuments and "Indiano" constructions. The region also has a history of Roman gold mining. Specific highlights include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/872539" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Marina de Buspol Chapel&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4515893" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ruins of the Fonfaraón Hospital&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Allande?
A: The mountain bike trails in Allande are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Reviewers often praise the diverse terrain and the scenic routes that traverse amazing landscapes, providing a peaceful and beautiful experience.

Q: Are there any long-distance mountain bike routes in Allande?
A: Yes, for those seeking longer rides, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1027482592/cangas-del-narcea-to-laguna-de-arbas-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cangas del Narcea to Laguna de Arbás Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 71.4 km route, typically taking over 7 hours to complete. This difficult trail offers extensive exploration through the region's varied landscapes.

Q: Can I find mountain bike routes that follow parts of the Primitive Way of St. James in Allande?
A: Yes, Allande encompasses stages of the Primitive Way of St. James. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1027482349/pola-de-allande-al-puerto-de-palo-y-la-marta-circular-en-el-camino-primitivo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pola de Allande al Puerto de Palo y La Marta – circular en el Camino Primitivo&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 47.9 km route that specifically traverses sections of this historic pilgrimage path, offering both scenic beauty and cultural insights.

Q: What is the highest point I might encounter on a mountain bike trail in Allande?
A: The region of Allande is characterized by a mountainous relief, with its highest point being Pico Panchón, reaching 1,410 meters. While specific trails might not go directly to the summit, you will experience significant elevation changes and ride through high mountain passes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2873224" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto de La Marta&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6493683" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto del Palo Pass&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any mountain bike routes that pass by reservoirs or large bodies of water?
A: Yes, you can find routes that pass by significant water features. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1027482692/grandas-de-salime-pasando-por-el-rio-vavia-ruta-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Grandas de Salime pasando por el río Vavia – ruta circular&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 37.3 km trail that takes you past the Río Vavia. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/872567" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Grandas de Salime Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; is a notable man-made monument in the vicinity.

Q: How many mountain bike trails are available in Allande?
A: There are 10 mountain bike trails available for exploration in Allande. These routes offer a range of experiences, predominantly catering to difficult skill levels.
tips: Shortly after leaving Pola de Allande, you leave the road and begin this spectacular stretch of the Camino. It is cyclable although, if it has rained, some points can be a bit slippery.
A village made up of four slate stone houses on the back of a mountain range almost a thousand meters above sea level. There is also a small chapel dedicated to Santiago, with an image of the saint.
It is an old water deposit that was used to extract gold. Cows and horses are often seen grazing on the banks, which gives this space an additional charm. From here there is a spectacular 360º view.

This is probably the best preserved hospital on the alternative route of the Camino Primitivo. It is located at an altitude of 1,190 meters, at the foot of the 1,292-meter Cimero peak. There is evidence that a town existed in the surroundings of this hospital thousands of years ago, in prehistoric times.
